page: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-24-lssd is a memory-optimized server instance featuring 24 vCPUs mapped to 12 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor running at 2.3 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it includes 186.0 GB of RAM, translating to 7.75 GB of memory per core. The instance features a massive 504 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for tile and int8 operations. Local storage is bundled, consisting of 1343 GB of NVMe SSD capacity across four local drives. No GPUs are included.

Benchmark data indicates strong single-core performance, with Geekbench single-core and text processing scores reaching the top 10% tier. Single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression also rank in the top 10%. Multi-core performance, measured via stress-ng and Passmark, falls into the average category, though Geekbench multi-core and image processing benchmarks show strong results in the top 25%. Memory performance is robust, with Passmark memory scores in the top 25%, while raw read/write memory bandwidth remains average. For specialized workloads, LLM inference on small models (135M) achieves top-tier text generation speeds, though performance drops to the bottom 10% when running larger 70B models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c4-highmem-24-lssd offers a high-density resource profile, balancing a substantial memory footprint with fast local NVMe storage without the overhead of dedicated GPUs. This makes it highly suitable for memory-intensive and storage-heavy workloads. Appropriate applications include database operations, caching layers like Redis, static web serving, software compilation, and text processing pipelines. The lack of GPU acceleration and poor large-model LLM inference speeds make it less suitable for heavy deep learning training or large-scale AI generation.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-24-lssd is a memory-optimized server configured with 24 vCPUs and 186.0 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor at 2.3 GHz. It features 1343 GB of local NVMe SSD storage across four drives, with no GPU acceleration. The x86_64 architecture supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX. Benchmarks show top-tier single-core CPU and text processing performance, alongside strong multi-core and image processing capabilities. While memory bandwidth and database operations perform at average levels, the instance excels at small-model LLM text generation and single-threaded compression. This server is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-dense and high-speed local storage tasks, making it highly suitable for database hosting, caching, static web serving, and text processing workloads.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-24-lssd is a memory-optimized server with 24 vCPUs, 186 GB RAM, and 1343 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier single-core and text processing performance.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-24-lssd features 24 vCPUs, 186 GB RAM, and 1343 GB NVMe SSD storage, optimized for memory-dense and database workloads.
tagline: A memory-dense server featuring high-speed local NVMe storage and advanced Intel matrix extensions for demanding database and text processing workloads.
bullet_points: 24 vCPUs and 186 GB RAM powered by Intel Xeon 6985P-C processors
1343 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage bundled
Top-tier single-core CPU, text processing, and compression performance
Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction set support
Well-suited for database operations, caching, and static web serving
